item_spec.concrete_change:
Add codex and antigravity as FreshSessionProvider implementations participating in provider resolution and best-effort quota, matching the existing provider pattern (see ClaudeCodeProvider: class with `name`, constructor(config, launchCommand=spawnLoggedCommand, opentoken), async launch(input)). Changes:

(1) NEW packages/audit-code/src/providers/codexProvider.ts: `export class CodexProvider implements FreshSessionProvider { name = "codex"; ... }`. launch() spawns the Codex CLI via the injected launchCommand (command default 'codex'; verify the actual non-interactive prompt flag during implementation — mirror ClaudeCodeProvider's promptFlag/extra_args handling and opentoken wrapping). Implement `async queryLimits(_model): Promise<ProviderRateLimits|null> { return null; }` best-effort per FINDING-003, with a comment that if Codex talks to an HTTP API exposing rate-limit headers, this is the natural place to issue a minimal probe and parse them (drop-in later). Guard against running inside an incompatible session if applicable.

(2) NEW packages/audit-code/src/providers/antigravityProvider.ts: `export class AntigravityProvider implements FreshSessionProvider { name = "antigravity"; ... }`. HONEST feasibility: Antigravity is an agentic IDE, not a headless CLI worker. Implement launch() to route through a configured command/task template when present (mirror VSCodeTaskProvider, which throws when vscode_task.command_template is missing); when no template is configured, throw a clear, actionable error like 'antigravity provider does not support headless dispatch without antigravity.command_template in session-config.json'. Implement best-effort queryLimits returning null with the same API-header hook comment.

(3) packages/audit-code/src/providers/index.ts: (a) import CodexProvider and AntigravityProvider; (b) in resolveFreshSessionProviderName's auto branch, add in-session detection rungs alongside insideClaudeCode/insideOpenCode — detect codex (e.g. an in-session env var if Codex sets one, else `codex` command availability) and antigravity (e.g. TERM_PROGRAM/an Antigravity env signal) — verify the actual signals during implementation; keep ordering deterministic and do not perturb existing claude-code/opencode/vscode detection; (c) add `case "codex": return new CodexProvider(sessionConfig.codex, undefined, opentoken);` and `case "antigravity": return new AntigravityProvider(sessionConfig.antigravity, opentoken);` to the createFreshSessionProvider switch (config field names per the new SessionConfig entries below).

(4) packages/audit-code/src/providers/constants.ts: add `export const CODEX_PROVIDER_NAME = "codex";` and `export const ANTIGRAVITY_PROVIDER_NAME = "antigravity";` (consistent with the existing LOCAL_SUBPROCESS_PROVIDER_NAME re-export) for callers that prefer constants over literals.

(5) packages/shared/src/providers/types.ts: no shape change to FreshSessionProvider/ProviderRateLimits is required (the new providers conform as-is); leave this file unchanged unless a shared CodexConfig/AntigravityConfig is preferred here rather than in sessionConfig.ts.

claude-desktop: do NOT add a separate provider/classification unless a distinguishing env signal exists — Claude Code running inside Claude Desktop sets CLAUDECODE and already resolves to claude-code. Add a distinct 'claude-desktop' classification only if Desktop is found to expose different limits/quota than the CLI; otherwise document that it folds into claude-code.

CROSS-FILE DEPENDENCIES (outside this finding's five files; the implement phase must include them or this will not typecheck): (i) shared/src/types/sessionConfig.ts — extend the ResolvedProviderName union with 'codex' and 'antigravity', extend the SessionConfig.provider type, and add optional CodexConfig/AntigravityConfig interfaces + `codex?`/`antigravity?` fields; (ii) shared/src/quota/limits.ts classifyProvider — add cases for 'codex' (likely 'hosted' if API-backed) and 'antigravity' (classify per its quota model) so wave ceilings are correct (coordinate with FINDING-004); (iii) CLAUDE.md provider list (coordinate with FINDING-007). This is a @audit-tools/shared change: rebuild shared and typecheck both dependents.

tests_to_write.name: provider resolution recognizes codex and antigravity
assertions: resolveFreshSessionProviderName('codex', {}) returns 'codex' and 'antigravity' returns 'antigravity' (explicit requests honored).
Under auto, the codex in-session/command signal resolves to 'codex' and the antigravity signal resolves to 'antigravity', via injected options.env/commandExists.
Existing detection (claude-code via CLAUDECODE, opencode via OPENCODE) is unchanged when the new signals are absent.

tests_to_write.name: createFreshSessionProvider constructs the new providers
assertions: createFreshSessionProvider('codex', sessionConfig) returns a CodexProvider with name 'codex'.
createFreshSessionProvider('antigravity', sessionConfig with a command template) returns an AntigravityProvider with name 'antigravity'.

tests_to_write.name: new providers behave best-effort and fail loudly only when unsupported
assertions: CodexProvider.launch builds the expected command/args via an injected launchCommand spy (no real process spawned).
AntigravityProvider.launch throws a clear, actionable error when no command/task template is configured.
queryLimits(null) resolves to null for both providers without throwing.
